AUTH.PASSWORD_INVALID: Password must be 8+ chars, include uppercase, a digit, and a special character.
AUTH.FORGOT_INSTRUCTIONS: Enter your email address and we'll send you a link to reset your password.
AUTH.FORGOT_SENT: If an account with that email exists, we've sent a password reset link. Please check your inbox.
AUTH.RESET_SUCCESS: Your password has been reset successfully. You can now sign in with your new password.
AUTH.PASSWORD_HINT: Min. 8 characters, uppercase letter, digit, and special character.

DASHBOARD.PENDING_MSG: Your account is awaiting admin approval. You'll get full access once an administrator activates your account.
DASHBOARD.NO_GROUPS: You have no groups assigned yet.
DASHBOARD.INVITE_DESC: Send an invitation to add a new evaluator.
DASHBOARD.MANAGE_USERS_DESC: Approve pending admins, manage roles and access.

EVENTS.TITLE: Events
EVENTS.NAME: Name
EVENTS.NO_AGE_BRACKETS: No age brackets configured. All participants will appear in a single "All" category.
EVENTS.CONFIRM_DELETE_EVENT: Delete event "{{name}}"? This will also delete all groups and participants.
EVENTS.CONFIRM_DELETE_GROUP: Delete group "{{name}}"? The group must be empty.
EVENTS.MUST_BE_IN_EVENT_POOL: Evaluator must be assigned to this event first
EVENTS.NO_EVALUATORS_IN_POOL: No evaluators assigned to this event yet
EVENTS.DRAG_EVENT_HINT: Drag an event onto an evaluator row to assign

IMPORT.TITLE: Import Event
IMPORT.MAP_INSTRUCTIONS: Map CSV columns to system fields
IMPORT.MAP_REQUIRED: You must map both "Participant Name" and "Group Name".

SCORING.NO_PARTICIPANTS: No participants found in your assigned group.

LEADERBOARD.TITLE: Leaderboard
LEADERBOARD.NO_AGE_BRACKETS_INFO: No age brackets are configured for this event. All participants are shown in a single category. Admins can add age brackets on the event detail page.
LEADERBOARD.NO_ACTIVITIES: No activities with records found for this event.
LEADERBOARD.NAME: Name

DIPLOMA.NO_FONTS: No custom fonts. Default Helvetica will be used.
DIPLOMA.ROBOTO_INFO: Roboto is bundled as the built-in default font — Czech characters (č, š, ž, ř, ů…) work automatically.
DIPLOMA.DEFAULT_FONT_NOTE: This font applies to all text items that have no explicit font assigned.
DIPLOMA.MOCK_NOTE: These values are only for preview and are not saved.
DIPLOMA.COORDS_NOTE: X and Y are percentages of page width/height
DIPLOMA.DRAG_HINT: Drag items on the preview to reposition them

REGISTER.PENDING_APPROVAL: Your account is pending approval by the administrator. You will be able to sign in once your account is activated.

ERRORS.SESSION_EXPIRED: Session expired. Please log in again.
ERRORS.EMAIL_EXISTS: This email address is already registered.
ERRORS.CSV_TOO_LARGE: CSV file exceeds the 5 MB limit.

ERRORS.CSV_NO_DATA: CSV file contains no data rows.
ERRORS.IMAGE_TOO_LARGE: Image file exceeds the 5 MB limit.
ERRORS.NOT_ASSIGNED_GROUP: You are not assigned to this group.
ERRORS.NOT_ASSIGNED_PARTICIPANT: You are not assigned to this participant's group.
ERRORS.EVALUATOR_ALREADY_ASSIGNED: Evaluator is already assigned to this group.
ERRORS.PAGE_NOT_FOUND: The page you're looking for doesn't exist.

LANDING.HERO_SUBTITLE: The all-in-one platform for organizing sports events, scoring participants, and generating instant leaderboards and diplomas.
LANDING.FEATURES_SUBTITLE: From importing participants to printing diplomas — all in one place.
LANDING.FEATURE_IMPORT_DESC: Import participants and groups from a spreadsheet in seconds with smart column mapping.
LANDING.FEATURE_AI_DESC: Snap a photo of a score sheet and let Gemini AI extract the results automatically.
LANDING.FEATURE_LEADERBOARD_DESC: Real-time rankings by age category and gender, with CSV export.
LANDING.STEP1_DESC: Upload a CSV with participants, groups, and metadata. Map columns and you're set.
LANDING.STEP2_DESC: Assign evaluators to groups and score activities — manually or with AI scan.
LANDING.STEP3_DESC: See leaderboards instantly, export data, and generate custom diplomas.
LANDING.ORG_GROUPS_DESC: Organize participants into groups with age brackets and metadata.
LANDING.ORG_EVALUATORS_DESC: Build an evaluator pool per event and assign them to groups with drag & drop.
LANDING.ORG_DIPLOMAS_DESC: Design custom diploma templates and batch-generate PDFs for all winners.
LANDING.DOCS_SUBTITLE: Check out our step-by-step guides for admins and evaluators.
LANDING.DOCS_ADMIN_DESC: Learn how to create events, manage groups, assign evaluators, and generate diplomas.
LANDING.DOCS_EVAL_DESC: Learn how to score participants, use AI photo scanning, and work offline.
LANDING.CTA_BOTTOM_SUBTITLE: Create a free account and import your first event in minutes.

DOCS_ADMIN.TITLE: Admin Guide
DOCS_ADMIN.SUBTITLE: Everything you need to organize and manage your events
DOCS_ADMIN.DASHBOARD_DESC: After logging in as an admin, you land on the dashboard — your central command center for managing all events. The dashboard displays quick-action cards at the top for creating a new event manually or importing one from a CSV file. Below the action cards, you'll see a complete list of all your events with their current status (Draft, Active, or Completed).
DOCS_ADMIN.DASHBOARD_DETAIL: Each event card shows the event name, date, number of groups, total participants, and how many activities are configured. You can click any event to open its detail page, where you can access the leaderboard, manage evaluators, edit the event setup, or generate diplomas. The dashboard also shows a greeting with your name and role badge, so you always know which account you're logged into. If your account is pending admin approval, a prominent banner will appear explaining the situation.
DOCS_ADMIN.DASHBOARD_TIP: Use the quick-action cards to jump straight into event creation. The 'Create Event' card opens the manual form, while 'Import CSV' launches the bulk import wizard — both paths create a draft event and redirect you to the setup wizard.
DOCS_ADMIN.DASHBOARD_STEP_1: Sign in with your admin credentials at the login page.
DOCS_ADMIN.DASHBOARD_STEP_2: You'll land on the dashboard showing all your events and quick-action cards.
DOCS_ADMIN.DASHBOARD_STEP_3: Click 'Create Event' to start a new event manually, or 'Import CSV' to bulk-upload.
DOCS_ADMIN.DASHBOARD_STEP_4: Click any event card to open its detail page for management.
DOCS_ADMIN.CREATING_EVENTS_DESC: There are two ways to create an event in Vör. The first method is the manual creation form, where you enter the event name, optional description, and event date. The second method is the CSV import wizard, which lets you upload a spreadsheet containing groups and participants in bulk. Both methods create the event in DRAFT status and automatically redirect you to the 4-step setup wizard.
DOCS_ADMIN.CREATING_EVENTS_DETAIL: When using the manual form, you only need to provide an event name — the description and date fields are optional. After submitting, the event is created immediately and you proceed to configure groups, activities, and evaluators in the setup wizard. The manual approach is best for smaller events or when you don't have participant data in a spreadsheet yet. For larger events with many groups and participants, the CSV import wizard saves significant time by populating everything from a single file.
DOCS_ADMIN.CREATING_EVENTS_TIP: You can always add more participants and groups later in the setup wizard, even if you start with a CSV import. The import creates the initial structure, and you fine-tune it in the setup steps.
DOCS_ADMIN.CREATING_EVENTS_STEP_1: Navigate to the dashboard and click 'Create Event' or 'Import CSV'.
DOCS_ADMIN.CREATING_EVENTS_STEP_2: For manual creation: fill in the event name, optional description, and date, then click Create.
DOCS_ADMIN.CREATING_EVENTS_STEP_3: For CSV import: upload your file, map columns, preview, and confirm.
DOCS_ADMIN.CREATING_EVENTS_STEP_4: You'll be redirected to the 4-step setup wizard to complete configuration.
DOCS_ADMIN.SETUP_WIZARD_DESC: The setup wizard is where you configure everything about your event before making it live. It consists of four sequential steps, each focused on a different aspect of event configuration. You can navigate freely between steps while the event remains in DRAFT status, allowing you to iterate on the setup until everything is perfect.
DOCS_ADMIN.SETUP_WIZARD_DETAIL: Step 1 — Groups & Participants: Create groups (like teams or stations) and add participants to each group. You can add participants individually by name or manage them in bulk. Step 2 — Activities & Age Categories: Define the activities that participants will be scored on, choose the scoring type for each, and optionally set up age brackets for filtered leaderboards. Step 3 — Evaluators: Assign evaluators from the event pool to specific groups using drag-and-drop. Each evaluator sees only their assigned group's participants. Step 4 — Review & Activate: See a summary of your entire configuration with validation warnings, then activate the event to go live. Once activated, evaluators can begin scoring immediately.
DOCS_ADMIN.SETUP_WIZARD_TIP: The Review step shows warnings if groups have no evaluators or no participants. Address these before activating to ensure a smooth scoring experience.
DOCS_ADMIN.SETUP_WIZARD_STEP_1: Step 1 — Groups: Add groups and populate them with participants.
DOCS_ADMIN.SETUP_WIZARD_STEP_2: Step 2 — Activities: Define scoring activities and age brackets.
DOCS_ADMIN.SETUP_WIZARD_STEP_3: Step 3 — Evaluators: Drag evaluators from the pool onto groups.
DOCS_ADMIN.SETUP_WIZARD_STEP_4: Step 4 — Review & Activate: Verify configuration and make the event live.
DOCS_ADMIN.CSV_IMPORT_DESC: The CSV import wizard provides a streamlined 3-step process for creating events with large numbers of participants. Instead of adding groups and participants one by one, you upload a single CSV file containing all the data. The wizard intelligently parses your file, lets you map columns to the required fields, and shows a preview before finalizing the import.
DOCS_ADMIN.CSV_IMPORT_DETAIL: Your CSV file should contain columns for group name, participant display name, and optionally external ID, gender (M/F), and date of birth. The wizard automatically detects common column names and pre-maps them for you. In the mapping step, you use dropdown selectors to assign each CSV column to the corresponding field. The preview step shows exactly how the data will be organized into groups and participants, letting you catch any issues before committing. After confirming, the event is created in DRAFT status with all groups and participants populated.
DOCS_ADMIN.CSV_IMPORT_TIP: Make sure your CSV uses UTF-8 encoding to properly handle special characters in names. The auto-mapper recognizes column headers like 'name', 'group', 'gender', 'dob', and 'external_id'.
DOCS_ADMIN.CSV_IMPORT_STEP_1: Click 'Import CSV' on the dashboard and upload your CSV file.
DOCS_ADMIN.CSV_IMPORT_STEP_2: Map CSV columns to required fields: group name, participant name, external ID, gender, date of birth.
DOCS_ADMIN.CSV_IMPORT_STEP_3: Review the preview showing how participants will be organized into groups.
DOCS_ADMIN.CSV_IMPORT_STEP_4: Confirm the import — the event is created in DRAFT status with all data populated.
DOCS_ADMIN.GROUPS_DESC: Groups are the organizational units within an event — they can represent teams, stations, classrooms, or any logical grouping of participants. Groups and their participants are managed in Step 1 of the setup wizard while the event is in DRAFT status. Each group can have any number of participants, and participants can be added, edited, or removed at any time during the draft phase.
DOCS_ADMIN.GROUPS_DETAIL: Each participant record includes a display name, an optional external ID (useful for matching with external systems), gender, and date of birth. The external ID can be used during AI photo scoring to match handwritten sheets with participant records. Groups are displayed as expandable accordion panels — click a group header to expand it and see its participants. You can rename groups inline by clicking the edit icon, or delete empty groups with the trash icon. To add a participant, expand a group and click the '+ Add Participant' link at the bottom. Once the event is activated, groups and participants become read-only on the event detail page, ensuring data integrity during live scoring.
DOCS_ADMIN.GROUPS_TIP: Use color identifiers for groups to help visually distinguish them in the setup wizard and on the event detail page. Groups imported via CSV will retain the exact structure from your spreadsheet.
DOCS_ADMIN.GROUPS_STEP_1: In Step 1 of the setup wizard, click '+ Add Group' to create a new group.
DOCS_ADMIN.GROUPS_STEP_2: Expand a group by clicking its header, then click '+ Add Participant' to add members.
DOCS_ADMIN.GROUPS_STEP_3: Use the edit icon (pencil) to rename a group inline, or the trash icon to remove empty groups.
DOCS_ADMIN.GROUPS_STEP_4: Click the trash icon next to a participant name to remove them from the group.
DOCS_ADMIN.EVALUATOR_ASSIGNMENT_DESC: Evaluator assignment in Vör follows a deliberate two-step process designed to give admins full control over who can access which data. First, you add evaluators to the event's evaluator pool from the Admin > Evaluators page. Then, in Step 3 of the setup wizard, you assign pool evaluators to specific groups using an intuitive drag-and-drop interface.
DOCS_ADMIN.EVALUATOR_ASSIGNMENT_DETAIL: The event pool acts as a gatekeeping layer — only evaluators who are in the pool can be assigned to groups within that event. This prevents accidental access. On the Admin > Evaluators page, you'll see a master list of all registered evaluators with checkboxes to toggle their pool membership for each event. In the setup wizard's Step 3, the unassigned pool evaluators appear at the top as draggable chips. Below that, each group has a drop zone where you can drag evaluators. Each evaluator can only be assigned to one group per event, ensuring clear responsibility. You can also click the × button on an assigned evaluator chip to move them back to the unassigned pool. The drag-and-drop interface supports both mouse and touch interactions for use on mobile devices.
DOCS_ADMIN.EVALUATOR_ASSIGNMENT_TIP: If an evaluator needs to be reassigned to a different group, remove them from their current group first (click ×), then drag them to the new group. The Review step will warn you about any groups that don't have an evaluator assigned yet.
DOCS_ADMIN.EVALUATOR_ASSIGNMENT_STEP_1: Go to Admin > Evaluators and add evaluators to the event pool.
DOCS_ADMIN.EVALUATOR_ASSIGNMENT_STEP_2: In the setup wizard Step 3, you'll see unassigned pool evaluators as draggable chips.
DOCS_ADMIN.EVALUATOR_ASSIGNMENT_STEP_3: Drag an evaluator chip from the pool and drop it onto a group's evaluator zone.
DOCS_ADMIN.EVALUATOR_ASSIGNMENT_STEP_4: To reassign, click × on the evaluator chip to return them to the pool, then drag to a new group.
DOCS_ADMIN.ACTIVITIES_DESC: Activities define what participants are scored on — for example, 'Long Jump', 'Sprint 100m', or 'Knowledge Quiz'. They are configured in Step 2 of the setup wizard alongside age categories. Each activity has a name and a scoring type that determines how evaluators enter scores and how the leaderboard ranks participants.
DOCS_ADMIN.ACTIVITIES_DETAIL: When creating an activity, you provide a name and select one of four scoring types. Numeric High is for activities where a higher number is better, like distance or points scored. Numeric Low is the opposite — lower values win, perfect for timed events or error counts. Boolean is a simple pass/fail toggle, useful for qualifying rounds or certification checks. Score Set allows you to define a predefined list of possible values, which evaluators select from a dropdown. Activities can be renamed or deleted at any time while the event is in DRAFT status. Each activity appears as a card showing its name and scoring type badge. The ordering of activities determines how they appear in the scoring view and leaderboard.
DOCS_ADMIN.ACTIVITIES_TIP: Choose the scoring type carefully — it cannot be changed after the event is activated. If you need both a timed event and a distance event, create two separate activities with the appropriate types.
DOCS_ADMIN.ACTIVITIES_NUMERIC_HIGH: Numeric High — Higher is better (e.g., distance, points)
DOCS_ADMIN.ACTIVITIES_NUMERIC_LOW: Numeric Low — Lower is better (e.g., time, errors)
DOCS_ADMIN.ACTIVITIES_SCORE_SET: Score Set — Predefined set of possible values
DOCS_ADMIN.AGE_CATEGORIES_DESC: Age categories (also called age brackets) allow you to segment participants on the leaderboard by age group. They are configured in Step 2 of the setup wizard alongside activities. Each category has a name, a minimum age, and a maximum age. When viewing the leaderboard, you can filter results by age category to see rankings within specific age groups.
DOCS_ADMIN.AGE_CATEGORIES_DETAIL: To create an age category, enter a name (like 'Juniors', 'Seniors', or '10-12'), then set the minimum and maximum ages. Participants are automatically assigned to categories based on their date of birth relative to the event date. Categories appear as colored chips that can be removed by clicking the × button. Age categories are optional — if you don't define any, the leaderboard shows all participants together regardless of age. You can define as many categories as needed, and they can overlap if desired (e.g., 'Under 15' and 'Under 18' can both include 14-year-olds).
DOCS_ADMIN.AGE_CATEGORIES_TIP: Make sure your min/max age ranges cover all expected participants. If a participant's age doesn't fall into any defined category, they will still appear in the overall leaderboard but won't show up in age-filtered views.
DOCS_ADMIN.LEADERBOARD_DESC: The leaderboard provides real-time rankings for each activity in your event. It automatically calculates positions based on the scoring type — sorting from highest to lowest for Numeric High activities, lowest to highest for Numeric Low, and counting passes for Boolean activities. Rankings update live as evaluators submit scores.
DOCS_ADMIN.LEADERBOARD_DETAIL: The leaderboard page includes filter controls for activity, gender, and age category. Use these to drill down into specific segments — for example, see the top female performers in the 'Juniors' age category for the 'Long Jump' activity. Gold, silver, and bronze medal indicators are displayed for the top three participants in each view. The CSV export button downloads the currently filtered leaderboard as a comma-separated file that opens directly in Excel, Google Sheets, or any spreadsheet application. The export includes participant name, group, score, rank, gender, and age category columns. Leaderboard data is cached for performance (5-minute TTL) and automatically refreshed when new scores are submitted.
DOCS_ADMIN.LEADERBOARD_TIP: Share the leaderboard URL with spectators — it works for any logged-in user with access to the event. The CSV export respects your current filters, so set the filters first, then export to get exactly the data you need.
DOCS_ADMIN.DIPLOMA_DESC: The diploma editor is a powerful visual tool for designing custom certificates and diplomas for your event participants. You can create multiple diploma templates per event, each with its own background image, custom fonts, and positioned text elements. The editor provides a live WYSIWYG preview that shows exactly how the final PDF will look.
DOCS_ADMIN.DIPLOMA_DETAIL: The editor has two main panels. The left panel contains all configuration options: template name, background image upload, page orientation (landscape or portrait), custom font uploads (.ttf/.otf files), mock preview values, and a list of positioned text items. The right panel shows a live preview of the diploma with all text items rendered at their configured positions. Each text item can be either DYNAMIC (pulling data like participant name, rank, activity, event name, date, gender, or age category) or STATIC (fixed text you type in). You position items by setting X/Y percentages, and can enable horizontal or vertical centering. Font size, weight (normal/bold/italic), color, and font family are all configurable per item. You can drag items directly on the preview to reposition them visually, with snap guides appearing at the 50% center lines. Multiple templates can be saved per event, and each can be selected from tabs at the top of the editor.
DOCS_ADMIN.DIPLOMA_TIP: Upload a background image first, then position your text items on top. Use the mock values panel to test how different names and placements will look. The built-in Roboto font works well for most cases, but you can upload custom .ttf or .otf fonts for branded diplomas.
DOCS_ADMIN.DIPLOMA_STEP_1: Navigate to the diploma editor from the event detail page.
DOCS_ADMIN.DIPLOMA_STEP_2: Upload a background image and choose landscape or portrait orientation.
DOCS_ADMIN.DIPLOMA_STEP_3: Click '+ Add Item' to add text elements. Choose DYNAMIC for participant data or STATIC for fixed text.
DOCS_ADMIN.DIPLOMA_STEP_4: Position items by dragging them on the preview or entering X/Y percentages.
DOCS_ADMIN.DIPLOMA_STEP_5: Customize font size, weight, color, and family for each item.
DOCS_ADMIN.DIPLOMA_STEP_6: Save the template. You can create additional templates using the '+ Add Template' button.
DOCS_ADMIN.USERS_DESC: The user management system controls who can access Vör and what they can do. There are three roles in the system: Super Admin (full system control), Admin (event management), and Evaluator (scoring only). User management is split across two pages: the Users page (super admin only) for role and activation control, and the Invitations page for sending email invites.
DOCS_ADMIN.USERS_DETAIL: On the Invitations page, admins can invite new users by entering their email address. The system sends an email with a unique setup link that expires after a set period. When the invitee clicks the link, they're taken to an account setup page where they provide their name and create a password. Super admins can choose the role (Admin or Evaluator) when sending invitations, while regular admins can only invite evaluators. The Invitations list shows all sent invitations with their status (pending or used), expiration date, and actions to resend or revoke. On the Users page (super admin only), you can see all registered users, change their roles via a dropdown, and activate or deactivate accounts. Deactivating a user prevents them from logging in without deleting their data. The system supports self-registration as well — users who register without an invitation are placed in a 'pending approval' state until an admin activates their account.
DOCS_ADMIN.USERS_TIP: When onboarding a large team of evaluators, send all invitations at once from the Invitations page. Each evaluator will receive their own unique setup link. You can resend invitations if someone hasn't received theirs, or revoke unused invitations to clean up.

DOCS_EVAL.TITLE: Evaluator Guide
DOCS_EVAL.SUBTITLE: How to score participants quickly and accurately
DOCS_EVAL.GETTING_STARTED_DESC: Welcome to Vör! As an evaluator, your role is to score participants during events. To get started, you need to set up your account using the invitation link sent to your email by an admin. If you registered on your own, your account will need to be approved by an admin before you can access the system.
DOCS_EVAL.GETTING_STARTED_DETAIL: When you receive the invitation email, click the setup link to open the account creation page. You'll see the email address you were invited with (pre-filled and locked) along with your assigned role. Enter your full name and create a password that meets the security requirements: at least 8 characters, including an uppercase letter, a digit, and a special character. After submitting, your account is immediately active and you can sign in. Your admin has already assigned you to specific groups within events, so after logging in you'll see your assigned work on the dashboard right away.
DOCS_EVAL.GETTING_STARTED_TIP: Bookmark the login page for quick access on event day. If you forget your password, use the 'Forgot password' link on the login page to receive a reset email.
DOCS_EVAL.GETTING_STARTED_STEP_1: Check your email for an invitation from Vör containing a setup link.
DOCS_EVAL.GETTING_STARTED_STEP_2: Click the link and fill in your full name and create a strong password.
DOCS_EVAL.GETTING_STARTED_STEP_3: Submit the form — your account is now active.
DOCS_EVAL.GETTING_STARTED_STEP_4: Sign in at the login page using your email and password.
DOCS_EVAL.DASHBOARD_DESC: Your dashboard is personalized to show only the events and groups that are relevant to you. After signing in, you'll see a greeting with your name and a list of event cards. Each card represents an event you're assigned to, showing the event name, your assigned group, the number of participants in your group, and the available activities to score.
DOCS_EVAL.DASHBOARD_DETAIL: Click on an event card to open the event detail page, where you'll see the full list of activities. Each activity shows its name and scoring type. Click on any activity to open the scoring view for that activity and your assigned group. If you're assigned to multiple events, all of them appear on your dashboard. The dashboard also shows your account status — if you're still pending admin approval, a banner will explain that you need to wait for activation before you can access scoring features.
DOCS_EVAL.DASHBOARD_TIP: On event day, the dashboard is your starting point. Open it, find your event, and click the activity you need to score. Everything is designed to get you from login to scoring in as few taps as possible.
DOCS_EVAL.SCORING_DESC: The scoring view is where you do your main work as an evaluator. After selecting an activity from the event detail page, you'll see a table listing all participants in your assigned group. Each row shows the participant's name, a score input field, and a status indicator (saved, unsaved, or empty). The input type adapts to the activity's scoring type.
DOCS_EVAL.SCORING_DETAIL: For Numeric High and Numeric Low activities, you'll see a number input field where you can type the participant's score (values between 0 and 99,999). For Boolean activities, a toggle switch lets you mark pass (green) or fail (gray) with a single tap. For Score Set activities, a dropdown lets you select from the predefined values. As you enter scores, the status column updates to show 'Unsaved' in yellow. When you're ready, click 'Save All' at the bottom to submit all scores at once. Successfully saved rows flash green briefly and show a 'Saved' status. If you navigate away with unsaved changes, a confirmation dialog will warn you to prevent accidental data loss.
DOCS_EVAL.SCORING_TIP: You don't need to fill in every participant's score before saving. The 'Save All' button only submits rows that have a value entered. You can come back later to fill in missing scores — previously saved scores will already be shown.
DOCS_EVAL.SCORING_STEP_1: From the event detail page, click on the activity you want to score.
DOCS_EVAL.SCORING_STEP_2: Enter scores for each participant using the appropriate input type.
DOCS_EVAL.SCORING_STEP_3: Check the status column — yellow 'Unsaved' means the score hasn't been saved yet.
DOCS_EVAL.SCORING_STEP_4: Click 'Save All' to submit all entered scores to the server.
DOCS_EVAL.SCORING_STEP_5: Rows will flash green and show 'Saved' to confirm successful submission.
DOCS_EVAL.AI_SCORING_DESC: Vör includes an AI-powered photo scanning feature that can read handwritten score sheets and automatically extract participant names and scores. This is especially useful during outdoor events where scores are recorded on paper first and need to be digitized quickly. The AI uses Google's Gemini vision model to analyze photos and match extracted data to your participant list.
DOCS_EVAL.AI_SCORING_DETAIL: To use AI scoring, open the scoring view for an activity and click the camera button (labeled 'AI Scan'). This opens your device's camera or file picker. Take a clear photo of the handwritten score sheet, making sure names and numbers are legible. The photo is automatically compressed to reduce upload size (max 1MB, max 1280px) before being sent to the server. While the AI processes the image, a loading overlay with a spinning indicator appears over the scoring table. Processing typically takes 5-15 seconds depending on the complexity of the sheet. The AI attempts to match extracted names to your participant list using fuzzy matching, so slight spelling variations are handled automatically.
DOCS_EVAL.AI_SCORING_TIP: For best results, photograph the score sheet in good lighting with the text clearly visible. Avoid shadows and ensure the camera is directly above the sheet. Print participant names on the sheet beforehand if possible — the AI is more accurate with printed names than cursive handwriting.
DOCS_EVAL.AI_REVIEW_DESC: After the AI processes your photo, a review modal appears showing the extracted results side by side with the original image. This is your chance to verify and correct any misread values before they're applied to the scoring table. The AI never saves scores directly — you always have the final say.
DOCS_EVAL.AI_REVIEW_DETAIL: The review modal displays the uploaded photo on the left and a list of parsed scores on the right. Each row shows the matched participant name and the extracted score value. You can edit any value directly in the review modal by clicking on it. If the AI couldn't match a name to any participant, it will show the raw text it extracted. Once you're satisfied with the values, click 'Confirm & Apply' to populate the scoring table with the reviewed values. The scores are applied as unsaved values — you still need to click 'Save All' in the scoring view to actually submit them. If the results are too inaccurate, click 'Discard' to throw away the AI results and start fresh with a new photo or manual entry.
DOCS_EVAL.AI_REVIEW_TIP: Always review the AI results carefully, especially for numeric scores where a misread digit can significantly affect rankings. The 'Confirm & Apply' button only fills in the scoring table — you still need to click 'Save All' separately.
DOCS_EVAL.OFFLINE_DESC: Vör is designed to work reliably in outdoor environments where internet connectivity may be spotty or unavailable. The offline mode ensures that you never lose scoring data, even if your device loses connection in the middle of entering scores. Scores are automatically saved to your device's local storage when the server is unreachable.
DOCS_EVAL.OFFLINE_DETAIL: When you click 'Save All' and the server is unreachable, scores are saved to IndexedDB on your device instead of being lost. An amber banner appears at the top of the scoring view showing how many scores are pending synchronization (e.g., '3 scores pending sync'). The banner includes a 'Sync Now' button that you can press once you're back online to manually trigger the upload. You can also continue entering and saving additional scores while offline — they'll all be queued for sync. When scores are successfully synced, the pending count decreases and the rows update to show 'Saved' status. The offline system is transparent — from your perspective, scoring works the same way whether you're online or offline. The only difference is the amber pending banner.
DOCS_EVAL.OFFLINE_TIP: If you know you'll be in an area with poor connectivity, don't worry about pre-loading data — the scoring view loads participant data when you first open it. Just make sure to open the scoring view while you still have connectivity, then you can score offline for as long as needed.
DOCS_EVAL.LEADERBOARD_DESC: The leaderboard shows live rankings for all scored activities. As an evaluator, you can view the leaderboard to see how participants in your group (and across all groups) are performing. Rankings are calculated automatically based on the activity's scoring type and update in real time as scores are submitted.
DOCS_EVAL.LEADERBOARD_DETAIL: The leaderboard page includes dropdown filters for activity, gender, and age category. Select an activity to see rankings for that specific activity, or switch between activities to compare performance across different events. Gender and age category filters let you view segmented rankings — for example, the top female participants in the 'Juniors' category. The top three participants in any view receive gold, silver, and bronze medal indicators. If diploma templates have been configured by an admin, you can download individual diplomas directly from the leaderboard by clicking the diploma icon next to a participant's name. The leaderboard is accessible to all users with event access, making it a great way to share results with spectators, coaches, and participants themselves.
DOCS_EVAL.LEADERBOARD_TIP: The leaderboard updates live — refresh the page or revisit it to see the latest scores from all evaluators. During an event, check the leaderboard periodically to spot any scoring anomalies early.

AI_REVIEW.TITLE: Review AI Results
AI_REVIEW.SUBTITLE: Check the parsed scores and edit if needed before applying.
AI_REVIEW.NO_SCORES: No scores were recognized. Try a clearer photo.
